message:
feat(mux): send keyboard and mouse-click input immediately, bypassing rAF batching

All interactive input (keystrokes, mouse clicks, touch taps, text input)
now sends directly on the control WebSocket instead of being queued for
the next requestAnimationFrame flush. This eliminates 0-16ms (~8ms avg)
of latency per interactive event. Small messages (<32 bytes) skip zstd
compression entirely (flag 0x00), avoiding overhead on tiny keystrokes.

Bulk/non-interactive messages (mouse move, scroll, resize, layout,
clipboard) remain rAF-batched for efficiency.
